title: Distributed Governance Research: A Functional Architecture for Selective Institutional Distribution (Working Paper v1.12)
description: Complete research corpus and working paper proposing a functional architecture for distributed public project governance: citizens allocate a mandate-recorded share of an existing public budget across verifiable projects under strict role separation, milestone-gated conditional disbursement, evidential contracts, and role-based reputation — distributed allocation within mandate-bound, visible, versioned, and contestable planning scopes. Includes formal mechanism-design propositions (incentive compatibility of milestone disbursement; collusion-proofness of protocolized fiscalization), a reproducible 10,000-agent simulation of eight experiments (six pre-registered), plus a later pre-registered symmetric selection-only stress test (NO-GO) that retires the earlier compound value-per-budget multiplier as a calibrated effect (the distributed selection advantage is real but small; the controlling specification is research/claim-and-estimand-contract.md), and a documented adversarial review record of forty-three attack briefs across five rounds with paired defenses and accepted resolutions, all propagated through the architecture corpus. The manuscript is drafts/paper.md (Spanish translation drafts/paper.es.md); the accessible companion is docs/101_CORE_V0_MINIMAL_PUBLISHABLE_MODEL.md.
notes: Dual-licensed repository: research corpus CC BY 4.0; manuscript and reviewer packets CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 pending publication venue; code MIT. See LICENSE.md.
